Internal Incident Report (Operational/Cyber) — Kingdom of Saudi Arabia — 2026-06-04 10:15 AST; Ref BT-CSIRT-2026-061.
Barq Transmissions JSC (CR 1012-5566100) reports suspected unauthorized export of issuer documents from the Issuer Disclosure Portal (IDP) prior to market release.
At 09:42 AST, Mr. Sami Al‑Hathloul (Treasury Analyst, DOB 1986-02-13, Riyadh HQ) using workstation EN‑RYD‑TR‑19 (SN RY‑88314, locale ar‑SA, header date 18‑11‑1447H) accessed “Q3 earnings guidance—draft” and “dividend scenarios—confidential”.
His AD username s.alhathloul86, company email s.alhathloul@barqtrans.jsc.sa, employee ID BT‑TR‑0442, and Iqama ending 4632 are recorded in the access log and HR file.
The session originated via VPN Gateway North (SessionID 7f2a9b1e‑11c3‑4ac1‑a9e2‑7d22c0b77f42, internal IP 10.44.19.22) minutes after a badge swipe at turnstile GHQ‑03 at 07:58.
Extracted files included a board‑secretariat agenda draft and a liquidity forecast; this non‑public issuer information may constitute MNPI under the Securities Regulator’s Market Conduct Division (SR‑MCD) framework.
One attachment referenced client Sahra Mining LLC (CR 4030‑7788123) and a supplier hedge roll plan; all names and CRs in the attachment are to be treated as confidential issuer data.
No external transfer has been confirmed; however, an attempted upload to object store bt‑report‑exports‑ry was blocked, and outbound to a foreign region triggered an ADAI cross‑border transfer check (permit request ADAI/TP/2026/1442 logged for contingency).
For PDPL compliance, personal data minimization has been applied, and any onward transfer outside the Kingdom will await the Authority for Data and AI (ADAI) guidance; a Senior Data Approval review is scheduled today at 14:00 AST.
Separately, web analytics show visitors aged 30–35 from Eastern Province using generic tablet model NX‑210 and AST timezone; these demographics are not tied to any name or account.
Another standalone log lists city “Jeddah”, device language Arabic, and browser build 112.0 for a session with no login, which by itself does not identify a person.
Ms. Rania Al‑Qattan (Compliance Officer) is coordinating issuer workflow remediation and SR‑MCD notice drafting; she is not implicated in the access.
Immediate actions: credentials for s.alhathloul86 were revoked, device certificate for EN‑RYD‑TR‑19 rotated, and the IDP export role “FIN_TREASURY_RW” temporarily disabled.
No trading halt has been requested at this time, but an embargo on external communications concerning Q3 performance remains in effect pending SR‑MCD confirmation.
Data subjects may contact data‑incident@barqtrans.jsc.sa or +966‑55‑123‑8890; regulators may use compliance@barqtrans.jsc.sa for secure liaison.
Root cause analysis and lessons learned will be finalized within 72 hours as required by PDPL incident timelines, with an update to the Board Risk Committee.
